title: Mildly Spicy Egyptian Potato Bake
description: A crumbly, peppery, show-off Egyptian dish featuring katsuramuki-cut russet potatoes, perfect for a dairy-free meal. Serve warm, garnished with fresh parsley.

ingredients.name: Kashkaval cheese
ingredients.notes: or substitute with feta or parmesan

instructions: Peel and slice the potatoes into katsuramuki-cut wedges
Heat the paprika oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes
Add the chopped garlic and cook for 1 minute
In a large bowl, combine the cooked onion and garlic mixture, potatoes, parsley, black pepper, cayenne pepper (if using), salt, and kashkaval cheese
In a separate bowl, mix together the tahini and lemon juice
Add the tahini-lemon mixture to the potato mixture and stir until well combined
Transfer the potato mixture to a terracotta dish and top with fresh breadcrumbs
Bake for 30 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the potatoes are tender
Serve warm, garnished with additional parsley if desired
